text.title: django-pytest Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-pytest as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Testing internal method calls instead of observable behavior.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tests that assert on _validate_items was called break on any refactoring that renames the method, even if behavior is unchanged. Test the outcome (exception raised, object created) not the path.

failure-mode.description: Shared mutable state on test class attributes (class-level list or dict).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tests execute in arbitrary order; shared state causes order-dependent failures. Use pytest fixtures — each test gets a fresh instance.

failure-mode.description: Re-creating fixtures inline instead of reusing shared conftest fixtures.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inline creation duplicates logic and diverges from the shared fixture over time. LLMs must read conftest.py first and reuse user, api_client, auth_client.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Masks migration drift — agents update models without re-running migrations, and existing DB state hides the missing column. CI must always --create-db.

failure-mode.description: Calling mocker.patch with the module the agent read, not the module where the SUT imports the symbol.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Patching the wrong import path produces a test that always passes regardless of the mock — silent zero coverage.
